metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Female
Highly competitive Agreeableness (13th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Highly disorganised Conscientiousness (4th pct) — Flexible, spontaneous, disorganised
Introverted Extraversion (27th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (80th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Highly conventional Openness to Experience (19th pct) — Conventional, practical, down-to-earth
  Very Low Tender-Mindedness (6th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Very Low Achievement Striving (3rd pct) — Easygoing, content with modest achievement
  Very Low Self-Discipline (0th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  Moderate Activity Level (41st pct)
  Very Low Excitement Seeking (14th pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (16th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  High Anger / Hostility (74th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  High Self-Consciousness (75th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (84th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Moderate Chronological Age (54th pct)

message.reasoning: We are to simulate a baseline opinion on "Trust_In_Mainstream_Journalism" for a 54-year-old female with specific personality traits. The profile shows high neuroticism (80th pct), low agreeableness (12.9th pct), low conscientiousness (4th pct), low openness (19th pct), and moderate extraversion (27th pct) but with low positive emotions and low excitement seeking. Key facets: very low trust (16.4th pct), very low compliance (17.3rd pct), high anger/hostility (74th pct), high anxiety (100th pct), low altruism (8.5th pct), low tender-mindedness (6.3rd pct). She is skeptical, emotionally reactive, and likely to view mainstream journalism with suspicion. Her conventionality (low openness) suggests she may prefer traditional sources but her low trust and high neuroticism may lead her to distrust media. She is also older (54) which may correlate with less trust in media generally. Given these traits, I expect a negative score, likely in the mild to clear opposition range. She is not extremely opposed (maximal) because she may still consume some mainstream news. But her low trust and high skepticism suggest a score around -400 to -600. Let's set score at -512, which is between -500 (clear opposition) and -600 (further into strong but not extreme). Confidence: 0.75, reasoning: low trust, high neuroticism, and low openness to new ideas contribute to skepticism; conventionality may not override distrust.
